issue_title: BigQuery Python Client v0.28.0: Can't recover {Dataset,Table}References from Dataset or Table objects
issue_body:
Hello,

In v0.28.0 of the BigQuery Python Client, it doesn't seem possible to recover the original `DatasetReference` or `TableReference` used to create the the `Dataset` or `Table`, thus preventing any part of the API (or a user's application) that requires a reference to also take the Dataset or Table object.  It seems the reference used in creating the Dataset or Table is lost on object creation [1][2].

For example, in the `client.load_table_from_file()` function [3], a `table_ref` is expected; it seems logically unreasonable that a full Table object can't be used to fulfil the need for a table reference.

Cheers,
Rob

[1] https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0.28.0/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/table.py#L170-L177
[2] https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0.28.0/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/dataset.py#L204-L208
[3] https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/0.28.0/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/client.py#L692-L706

issue_title: "expiresOn" key in returned token seems to be wrong
issue_body:
The token returned by the library contains another expiry time as the JSON returned from the same request suggests. I think this part of oauth2_client.py is responsible for this:

```        
if expires_in:
            now = datetime.now()
            soon = timedelta(seconds=expires_in)
            wire_response[OAuth2.ResponseParameters.EXPIRES_ON] = str(now + soon)
```

"expiresOn" is calculated locally and therefore does not match the "expires_on" in the JSON, the difference being in seconds.

issue_title: doc: python-howto un-silence pytest
issue_body:
came up in a mail thread, and I think it's come up before:
https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/e5TGsO843OM

This is a pytest thing, to unsuppress stdout/stderr in pytest you need 
to tell it `-s`, so: 

New style: 
$ ./pants goal test --test-pytest-options=-s [target] 

Old style: 
$ ./pants [target] -s

issue_title: The Depmap task should have its IDEA specific bits split out
issue_body: Right now [Depmap](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/depmap.py) requires manual invocation of `resolve depmap` whenever the `--project-info` option is specified.  This appears to be an attempt to not foist resolve on normal human users of the `depmap` goal but still allow the IDEA plugin to call the goal and get the resolved data it needs.  Instead of this split behavior, it would be better to split this into 2 tasks.

issue_title: Make 3rdparty source roots a first-class thing
issue_body: Right now we treat 3rdparty like "source" (in the sense of "source" vs "test"), but it should be a third kind of source root, a sibling concept to source and test. This will make things like GoBuildgen work without hacks.

issue_title: isolated strategy - compile targets with user changes first
issue_body:
Apologies if this has been fixed since whatever version of pants Foursquare is currently on...

Especially while working on low level libs, it's helpful to compile targets with user changes (ones that have diverged from origin/master) first. Example:

B depends on A, C depends on B, D depends on B. E-Z depend on B. I change B and C.

Trivially, A has to be compiled first. I'd like for B and C to be next. In practice, it's far more likely that they'll have broken than D-Z and seeing those errors first will help iteration speed.

issue_title: Implement "affected files" metric via rust `Graph`
issue_body:
As mentioned in `context.py`, we need to port computation of the file count metric to using the rust `Graph` API:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/goal/context.py#L181-L186

This will help to unblock #5639 by providing an implementation of this useful metric that does not require any `Target` objects to be created.

----

Doing this will involve a few steps.
1. Add an API to `src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs` that given a set of `NodeKey` roots, returns the count of `DigestFile` Nodes below those roots. This will involve using `Graph::walk` ([example](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/b3f7124c9076be3bf4e1cb369bfeda16f3b963f6/src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs#L218-L221)) and some of the logic from [Graph::all_digests](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/b3f7124c9076be3bf4e1cb369bfeda16f3b963f6/src/rust/engine/src/graph.rs#L456-L472) in a new method that walks and counts digests it encounters.
2. Expose the new `Graph` API from (1) in `src/rust/engine/src/lib.rs`. The signature there would probably be something like: ```
#[no_mangle]
pub extern "C" fn graph_count_file_digests(
  scheduler_ptr: *mut Scheduler,
  execution_request_ptr: *mut ExecutionRequest,
) {
```. The `ExecutionRequest` would hold the root nodes to operate on.
3. Update [src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L302) to record all root `Nodes` that have been requested via [LocalScheduler::execution_request](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L378-L399) since the `Scheduler` was created. Reset the recorded roots in [pre_fork](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L414).
4. Link everything up by adding a method to `LocalScheduler` that uses the recorded roots to create an `ExecutionRequest`, call your native `graph_count_file_digests` method, and return the count of reachable file digests.
5. Finally, replace the body of [_set_affected_target_files_count_in_runtracker](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7ffc11595f58711e5074629eb5efe4e79bfc4809/src/python/pants/goal/context.py#L181-L186) with a call to your `LocalScheduler` method.

issue_title: the native toolchain binaries are taking up a lot of bandwidth and require a new approach
issue_body:
from @benjyw on `#general` just now:

> So, our S3 bandwidth costs went through the roof last month, and it’s mostly because of clang and gcc:
```
936.9GB 3452 bin/clang/linux/x86_64/6.0.0/clang.tar.gz
785.9GB 3401 bin/gcc/linux/x86_64/7.3.0/gcc.tar.gz
537.2GB 6987 bin/go/linux/x86_64/1.7.3/go.tar.gz
292.0GB 6891 bin/protobuf/linux/x86_64/3.4.1/protoc
195.9GB 6981 bin/cmake/linux/x86_64/3.9.5/cmake.tar.gz
187.8GB 5029 bin/thrift/linux/x86_64/0.9.2/thrift
183.1GB 5553 bin/watchman/linux/x86_64/4.9.0-pants1/watchman
```
> These are the top few downloads, by cumulative # of bytes (the middle number is the # of downloads)
> It seems not great for us to be hosting clang and gcc. Are these custom tarballs, or could we have users download them directly from… wherever one does that from?
> Ditto go, that one seems ~straightforward

Because this is driving up bandwidth costs as we speak, it's relatively high priority. Notes:

- We should just download the go distribution from the go download page.
- clang for osx is an unmodified binary download, this can be replaced verbatim.
- gcc for osx *may* be available as a binary download through homebrew. The idea here would be to pull a binary download from the homebrew repos, not to invoke the homebrew command. If homebrew actually builds gcc locally when installed (I will check this), we may need to do the same unless there is a trustworthy alternative.
- There are no official binary releases of clang or gcc for linux. We already have scripts to build them locally, which could be applied here -- alternatively, we can try to download a binary package from one of the linux package manager repositories (again, without invoking any package managers).
- Worst case, for all of the largest binary packages, we do as we already are doing in `xcode_cli_tools.py` and find and use the installed tools, or error out with installation instructions if they could not be found.

All the reasons to provide binaries in general are still valid (e.g. reproducibility), especially for the native toolchain, because the versions and configurations of `gcc` and `clang` may vary wildly across linux distributions. However, we can address that in followup work after we fix this issue.

issue_title: Wait() should return normally if the child process has already terminated
issue_body:
Calling `wait()` on a PtyProcess object whose process has already terminated will raise an exception. This behavior is unexpected[1].
I have made a small [test](https://gist.github.com/reynir/6616b43999449f26d299) to show case the behavior. Using `p.isalive() && p.wait()` will only make the race condition worse in my tests.

[1]: subprocess.Popen.wait() returns normally even when the process has already terminated, although it doesn't say so explicitly in the documentation. In [java](http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Process.html#waitFor%28%29) the `waitFor()` method *"returns immediately if the subprocess has already terminated."*

issue_title: nifgen methods that have "waveform handle" and "waveform name" should use fancy wrapper
issue_body:
For `nifgen.Session.write_waveform`, we created a _Fancy Wrapper_ in which a parameter is either the waveform handle or the waveform name.

We found two instances that could and should do the same for consistency.

* `set_waveform_next_write_position` and `set_named_waveform_next_write_position`
* `clear_arb_waveform` and `delete_named_waveform`

The `next_write_position` methods are very niche, so we could just stop code-generating until a compelling use case arises. If that happens, we should make sure it's tracked via an issue.

Our package (`datadotworld`), depends on `datapackage-py<1.0a` (pre-v1).
The release `0.8.9` is causing us major issues due to transitive dependency on pre-release versions of other packages.

Specifically, because it depends on `tabulator<1.0.0a`, unless my users install my package using `--pre` nothing works and they are stuck with a `VersionConflict` error.

Please update `datapackage-py` (pre-v1) to eliminate dependencies on pre-release packages, or release `tabulator 1.0.0` as a full release.

Thanks!

issue_title: Short (<1 day) durations not supported well
issue_body:
`isodate` will return `datetime.timedelta` objects for periods shorter than 1 day, instead of an `isodate.Duration` instance.
However, `cast_duration` only checks for the latter and not the former, which results in exceptions when trying to re-cast an already casted value.

issue_title: swarm build should be optional
issue_body: At least for consistency with optional Mesos and k8s, where we don't build any components by default, it should probably require `make -C swarm/ build` to build the swarm scheduler components.

issue_title: Proposal: containerize the development environment
issue_body:
Currently to hack on, build, or test _all_ of Deis, there's no shortage of things that need to be installed into the _local_ test environment.  This includes Docker, Go, Python, PostgreSQL, shellcheck and more.  This can be daunting to set up for a new contributor.  Even seasoned contributors can have some difficulty transitioning to a new machine or after upgrading their OS.

What about containerizing the whole shooting match?  This could lower the barrier to just Docker and make.

issue_title: Docs should live natively on docs.globus.org instead of being linked to
issue_body:
This is for when we come fully out of beta.

We currently link to https://globus.github.io/globus-cli/ from https://docs.globus.org/cli/ -- the new CLI documentation would probably replace https://docs.globus.org/cli/ and that would need to be moved to `/hosted-cli` or `/legacy-cli` or something similar.

issue_title: Matplotlib PyQt5 DLL load failed: The specified procedure could not be found

issue_title: How to solve system env and virtual env conflict on different version of opencv?
issue_body:
I have installed opencv 3.4.1 `correctly` on my system environment Ubuntu 16.04. i.e. `python3, import cv2`, and `cv2.__version__` gives me correct version output.

But after I create a conda virtual environment using a **yml file inlcues opencv3.2.0**, it leads some problems here:

**Under the conda virtual env**, I have to always first

`cd myenv/lib/python3.5/site_packages/`

such that 
```
python3
import cv2
```
 is correctly using the 3.2.0 version of opencv, `without this step`, 3.4.1 will be used.

**Under the conda base environment** , if the PATH in ~/.bashrc is added for anaconda3, then I will under conda base enviroment by default every time open the terminal. 

And
```
python3
import cv2 
cv2.__version__
```
will tell me opencv 3.4.1 version is used.

**Under the system enviroment**, i.e first delete the PATH to anaconda3, then` import cv2` will lead to segmentation fault.

How to solve this problem? Thank you.

issue_title: cache_fn_url() changed in 4.3.x
issue_body:
In conda < 4.3 the function was simply:
```
def cache_fn_url(url):
    md5 = hashlib.md5(url.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
    return '%s.json' % (md5[:8],)
```
however, in 4.3, an additional `url = url.rstrip('/')` has been added.  Therefore the resulting filename is different.

This means that updating to conda 4.3.2 will automatically invalidate all existing caches of `repodata` from any channel.  I'm not sure if this is going to be a big problem, but I assume that people who operate in offline mode are going to suddenly not be able to do operations which involve rely on having a local cache of repodata.  I'm thinking about air-gapped environments.  The easiest solution would be to restore the old functionality.

issue_body:
If I execute a command like:

$ ssh -t dev-vz-2.devcio.com "sudo /opt/anaconda/bin/conda install --yes --quiet conda-api=1.0.0"

```

or if multiple packages are being installed, then output

```
SUCCESS conda-api 1.0.0
SUCCESS foo-pkg 1.2.3
```

I mention this because sometimes when executing conda through sudo, a script, via ssh, or in fabric, the screen output is the only (easy) way to see what happened, so exit codes aren't there.

issue_title: Support of resuming download
issue_body:
If a download is interrupted half way through, there is no way to continue the download. If the download take to much time it will be re-started from scratch. Worse, if an install of multiple packages is interrupted, all packages will be downloaded again, even if it last only one package.

For those who have to work with mobile internet connection, it's painful.

issue_title: conda-build reports missing dependencies incorrectly
issue_body:
@daler commented on [Wed Oct 12 2016](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1454)

I think `conda-build` is reporting the wrong thing when it hits a certain kind of dependency conflict. This gets kind of complicated so I'll first describe it in general terms and then show a reproducible example.

## The general problem

- A depends on B and C
- B depends on C
- A is to be built for `CONDA_R=3.3.1`
- B only exists in channels for `CONDA_R=3.2`. So `conda-build` should report a dependency conflict when building A
- C exists in channels for `CONDA_R=3.3.1` and `CONDA_R=3.2`

When I try building A, I expect to get a dependency conflict error saying that B doesn't exist for `R=3.3.1`. But instead I get a dependency conflict error saying that C doesn't exist for `R=3.3.1` (when in fact it does).

## Working example

### `conda info` on relevant packages
This package `bioconductor-annotationdbi` (package "C" in the example above), is built for both R3.2 and R3.3:

```
bioconductor-annotationdbi 1.32.3 0
-----------------------------------
file name   : bioconda::bioconductor-annotationdbi-1.32.3-0.tar.bz2
dependencies:
    bioconductor-biobase >=1.17.0
    bioconductor-biocgenerics >=0.15.10
    bioconductor-iranges
    bioconductor-s4vectors
    r >=2.7.0
    r-dbi
    r-rsqlite


bioconductor-annotationdbi 1.34.4 r3.3.1_1
------------------------------------------
file name   : bioconda::bioconductor-annotationdbi-1.34.4-r3.3.1_1.tar.bz2
dependencies:
    bioconductor-biobase >=1.17.0
    bioconductor-biocgenerics >=0.15.10
    bioconductor-iranges
    bioconductor-s4vectors >=0.9.25
    r 3.3.1*
    r-dbi
    r-rsqlite
```

This package `bioconductor-genomicfeatures` ("B" in the example above) is not yet built for R3.3.1. Note that it has a dependency for `bioconductor-annotationdbi`:

```
bioconductor-genomicfeatures 1.22.13 r3.2.2_0
---------------------------------------------
file name   : bioconda::bioconductor-genomicfeatures-1.22.13-r3.2.2_0.tar.bz2
dependencies:
    bioconductor-annotationdbi >=1.27.9
    bioconductor-biobase >=2.15.1
    bioconductor-biocgenerics >=0.1.0
    bioconductor-biomart >=2.17.1
    bioconductor-biostrings >=2.23.3
    bioconductor-genomeinfodb >=1.5.16
    bioconductor-genomicranges >=1.21.32
    bioconductor-iranges >=2.3.21
    bioconductor-rtracklayer >=1.29.24
    bioconductor-s4vectors >=0.7.17
    bioconductor-xvector
    r 3.2.2*
    r-dbi
    r-rcurl
    r-rsqlite
```


### the problematic `meta.yaml`

This `meta.yaml` (package "A" in the example) illustrates the problem.

```yaml
package:
  name: demo
  version: 0.1
requirements:
  build:
    - bioconductor-annotationdbi
    - bioconductor-genomicfeatures
    - r
```

When I try building it, it says that `bioconductor-annotationdbi` conflicts:

```
$ conda build . --channel r --channel bioconda
BUILD START: demo-0.1-0
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/linux-64
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/noarch
Using Anaconda Cloud api site https://api.anaconda.org
...INFO:stderrlog:...
Can't build /bioconda-recipes/tmp due to unsatisfiable dependencies:
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - bioconductor-annotationdbi
  - r 3.3.1*
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.
```

I interpreted this to mean that `bioconductor-annotationdbi` does not exist for R3.3.1. But in fact it does -- and it's even found by `conda info`.

Curiously, if I comment out the line for `bioconductor-genomicfeatures`, it works. Note that I'm commenting out the package *not* listed as a dependency conflict:

```yaml
package:
  name: demo
  version: 0.1
requirements:
  build:
    - bioconductor-annotationdbi
    # - bioconductor-genomicfeatures
    - r
```

```
$ conda build . --channel r --channel bioconda
...
# Automatic uploading is disabled
# If you want to upload this package to anaconda.org later, type:
#
# $ anaconda upload /miniconda3/conda-bld/linux-64/demo-0.1-0.tar.bz2
#
# To have conda build upload to anaconda.org automatically, use
# $ conda config --set anaconda_upload yes

```
### Finally:

So when `conda-build` reported the conflict:

```
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - bioconductor-annotationdbi
  - r 3.3.1*
```

shouldn't it instead be reporting that `bioconductor-genomicfeatures` is the conflicting package?


---

@daler commented on [Wed Oct 12 2016](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1454#issuecomment-253324069)

### automatically discovered dependency is also not found after it's built

I would also like to add that if I put the `bioconductor-annotationdbi` recipe in an adjacent folder to the example recipe above, `conda-build` sees it and tries to build it. But even after building it successfully, it is not successfully identified as a dependency:

```
BUILD START: demo-0.1-0
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/linux-64
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/noarch
Using Anaconda Cloud api site https://api.anaconda.org
...INFO:stderrlog:...
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - bioconductor-annotationdbi
  - r 3.3.1*
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.
Missing dependency bioconductor-annotationdbi, but found recipe directory, so building bioconductor-annotationdbi first


... (bioconductor-annotationdbi builds successfully here)

TEST END: bioconductor-annotationdbi-1.34.4-r3.3.1_1
INFO:/miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda_build/config.py:--keep-old-work flag not specified.  Removing source and build files.

# Automatic uploading is disabled
# If you want to upload this package to anaconda.org later, type:
#
# $ anaconda upload /miniconda3/conda-bld/linux-64/bioconductor-annotationdbi-1.34.4-r3.3.1_1.tar.bz2
#
# To have conda build upload to anaconda.org automatically, use
# $ conda config --set anaconda_upload yes


... but then:


BUILD START: demo-0.1-0
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/linux-64
updating index in: /miniconda3/conda-bld/noarch
...INFO:stderrlog:...
Can't build /bioconda-recipes/tmp due to unsatisfiable dependencies:
The following specifications were found to be in conflict:
  - bioconductor-annotationdbi
  - r 3.3.1*
``` 

---

@msarahan commented on [Thu Oct 13 2016](https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1454#issuecomment-253517732)

Thanks for reporting.  Any complaint with conda's hints should be posted to the conda issue tracker, though.  Conda-build is just using conda, and has no control over this behavior.

I'm moving this issue to the conda issue tracker.

issue_title: R Cairo library font problem
issue_body:
Hi -

I installed both R-3.3.1 and Microsoft R Open 3.2.3 using the conda package manager and cannot get fonts to render properly in plots for which the Cairo library backend is used (`CairoPDF`, `CairoPNG`, etc.). For example:

`
library(ggplot2)
library(Cairo)

# Set up the plot
pl = qplot(data = mtcars, x = cyl, y = mpg)

# Export using default pdf function
pdf(file = 'test_plot_default.pdf', height = 6, width = 6)
print(pl)
dev.off()

# Export using Cairo pdf function
CairoPDF(file = 'test_plot_Cairo.pdf', height = 6, width = 6)
print(pl)
dev.off()
`

I have confirmed this issue on both Ubuntu 14.04 and 16.04, as well as similarly bad results when using the `CairoPNG` function instead of `png`.

[test_plot_Cairo.pdf](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/445879/test_plot_Cairo.pdf)
[test_plot_default.pdf](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/445878/test_plot_default.pdf)

issue_title: Best way to stream large datasets into an on-disk bcolz array
issue_body:
I have a large dataset between 300G - 500G. The shape of this dataset is about (4000, 60000, 1000). As you can see, I don't have many rows but I have a very large number of columns and multiple dimensions. Couple of questions,

1) What bcolz settings should I test out for data of this shape?
2) What is the best way to stream data of this kind, row by row, into a carray? 
3) Can the streaming be done using multiple threads or processes?

Right now, I am using something like the following:

```
numpy_arrays = [list of 4000 numpy arrays]
shape = (4000, 60000, 1000)
arr = bcolz.zeros(shape=shape, dtype=dtype, rootdir=tmp_dir + 'data.bcolz')
def append(i, numpy_array):
  arr[i] = numpy_array
ThreadPool(NUM_THREADS).map(append, enumerate(numpy_arrays))
```

Really appreciate your time and brain space!

issue_title: An issue with writing from multiprocessing queue.
issue_body:
Hello!
I am trying to follow this thread for multiprocess writing in a file: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/13446445/python-multiprocessing-safely-writing-to-a-file

There seems to be a problem with the modification I wrote using pysam:

```python
import argparse
import pysam
import multiprocessing as mp

# Parse arguments
parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument('--bam', action='store', dest='bam',
                    help='Input bam for filtration')
parser.add_argument('--bed', action='store', dest='bed',
                    help='Input BED with intervals. The read initial alignment should fall in the interval.')
parser.add_argument('--threads', action='store', dest='threads',
                    help='Number of multiprocessing threads.')
args = parser.parse_args()


in_bam = pysam.AlignmentFile(args.bam, mode='rb')
out_name = '.'.join(args.bam.split('/')[-1].split('.')[:-1]) + '.match.bam'
out_bam = pysam.AlignmentFile(out_name, mode='wb', template=in_bam)
in_bam.close()


def listener(q):
    '''listens for messages on the q, writes to file. '''
    while 1:
        print(q.qsize())
        reads = q.get()
        print('Got reads!')
        if reads == 'kill':
            #f.write('killed')
            break
        #print('Reads,',reads)
        for read in reads:
            print('tokom',read)
            out_bam.write(read)
        print('pre')
        out_bam.flush()
        print('posle')
    out_bam.close()

def fetch_reads(line, q):
    parts = line[:-1].split('\t')
    #print(parts)
    start,end = int(parts[1])-1,int(parts[2])-1
    in_bam = pysam.AlignmentFile(args.bam, mode='rb')
    fetched = in_bam.fetch(parts[0], start, end)
    reads = [read for read in fetched]
    in_bam.close()
    if reads:
        #print(reads)
        q.put(reads)
        print('Done!')
    return reads

#must use Manager queue here, or will not work
manager = mp.Manager()
q = manager.Queue()
if not args.threads:
    threads = 1
else:
    threads = int(args.threads)
pool = mp.Pool(threads+1)

#put listener to work first
watcher = pool.apply_async(listener, (q,))
    
with open(args.bed,'r') as bed:
    jobs = []
    cnt = 0
    for line in bed:
        # Fire off the read fetchings
        job = pool.apply_async(fetch_reads, (line, q))
        jobs.append(job)
        cnt += 1
        if cnt > 300000:
            break

# collect results from the workers through the pool result queue
print('Begin get...')
for job in jobs: 
    job.get()
    #print('get')

#now we are done, kill the listener
q.put('kill')
pool.close()
```

Please note this is a debugging version so it is somewhat dirty. The output is:
0
Begin get...
Got reads!
('tokom', <pysam.calignedsegment.AlignedSegment object at 0x7f5efd219db8>)
Done!
Done!
Done!
Done!

It would seem that the write in pysam never completes. Can you lend a hand?
Also, is there a recommendation for writing in multiprocessing? I've seen many threads on reading with multiple processes, but not about writing.
Thanks in advance!

issue_title: pysam sort removes last x sequences
issue_body:
Dear developers, 
I've discovered an issue with pysam.sort() where the last x lines are discarded from the sorted output. Even when you provide a file with only for example 2 lines no output will be generated in the sorted file. 

This has been verified in several cases. 
As an example I've included a google drive link containing a sam file with only 2 lines, when converted to BAM, these 2 lines are still present, however, when sorting using your pysam tool, a blank file is generated. 

https://drive.google.com/open?id=0B3z-MmiBk77oNld0cDNDMnJuOHc

Executing the conversion and sorting with samtools (not pysam) does not generate these problems. If you look at the size of the ouput files, the pysam files are always smaller as they exclude the last x lines.

issue_title: can't use flush to get id of inserted rows
issue_body:
Note: at first this looks like #25 but it's not.

When attempting to more complex inserts as a single transaction SQLAlchemy I get an error:

```
sqlalchemy.exc.InternalError: (psycopg2.InternalError) SAVEPOINT not supported except for COCKROACH_RESTART
```

All I'm doing is inserting a row and then calling `flush()` so I can get the PK value to use in the next insert, e.g.

```python3
    def callback(session):
        """the transaction"""
        dataset = Dataset({'uuid': obj['header']['Our Ref:'],
                           'user_ref': obj['header']['Your Ref:'],
                           'manifest_type': obj['type'],
                           'manifest_version': obj['version'],
                           'manifest_json': raw_json})
        session.add(dataset)
        session.flush() # need dataset.id

        session.add(Dataset_user({'id_dataset': dataset.id,
                                  'id_user': user_id,
                                  'id_user_class': user_class.id}))

    sessionmaker = sqlalchemy.orm.sessionmaker(db.engine)
    run_transaction(sessionmaker, callback)
```

Possible I've missed something obvious so apologies in advance if that's the case.

issue_title: [ResourceManager] Issues with Lock commands
issue_body:
There are a number of serious usability issues with the `az lock` commands:

- [ ] I could not create a lock on my VNET using the `az lock create` command. It says: `The resource type could not be found in the namespace 'Microsoft.Network' for api version '2015-01-01'.` It could be using the API version of the lock API but needing the API version of the virtualNetworks resource. The `az resource` commands handle this behind the scenes.
- [ ] `--parent-resource-path` is listed as optional, but if you omit it, it says "Parameter 'parent_resource_path' can not be None." You have to specify ''...
- [ ] The parameter names for specifying the resource are inconsistent with the `az resource` commands. Specifically: `az lock: --parent-resource-path, --resource-provider-namespace` are inconsistent with `az resource: --namespace, --parent`.
- [ ] The `ids` parameter is not supported for either the lock itself or the target resource. It should be supported for both. 
- [ ] in `az resource`, `--resource-type` accepts the type or namespace/type. `az lock` does not do this.
- [ ] No short summaries for any of the commands
- [ ] `az lock update` has parameters with no help text

issue_title: webapp deployment source config-zip use async api
issue_body:
Currently the `az webapp deployments source config-zip` command uses the sync version of the api endpoint `api/zipdeploy` on the webapp

This results in a small issue when deploying this way, because apparently the load balancer in front of the `.scm.azurewebsites.net` has a request timeout of 230 seconds, as mentioned by David Ebbo [in this issue on kudu](https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/issues/2474).

We have quite a few times run into an issue where this leads to us getting a status code 500 back from a deploy, while the deploy was still finishing in the web app.

We have in our projects changed away from using the azure cli, to using a hand crafted bash script that uses the async version of the api as referenced in the documentation [here](https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/wiki/REST-API#zip-deployment).

I think the `az webapp deployments source config-zip` command could benefit from using the `?isAsync=true` query parameter, to stop getting unnecessary and untrue errors.

issue_title: The server side paging scenario in storage commands is not completed.
issue_body:
The `az storage blob list`, `az storage container list` and `az storage share list` commands all exposed `--marker` and `--num-results` parameters for server-side paging. However, the scenario is never completed because the `next_marker` property is not returned.

The `next_maker` is an opaque string for the next command to retrieve the next page of results, it is the value served to `--marker` parameter. To expose this value, a change is required for the list commands' results. The current return type of list command is a JSON list.

#### Solution 1:
Change the results type from list to dictionary:
```JSON
{
  "items": [
    {
      "content": null,
      "metadata": null,
      "name": "file_1016.txt",
      "properties": {
        "appendBlobCommittedBlockCount": null,
        "blobType": "BlockBlob",
        "contentLength": 264,
        "contentRange": null,
        "contentSettings": {
          "cacheControl": null,
          "contentDisposition": null,
          "contentEncoding": null,
          "contentLanguage": null,
          "contentMd5": "lMf6lT+DBK3I6k9YdydOgg==",
          "contentType": "application/octet-stream"
        },
        "copy": {
          "completionTime": null,
          "id": null,
          "progress": null,
          "source": null,
          "status": null,
          "statusDescription": null
        },
        "etag": "0x8D4B758188BCA6C",
        "lastModified": "2017-06-19T21:13:56+00:00",
        "lease": {
          "duration": null,
          "state": "available",
          "status": "unlocked"
        },
        "pageBlobSequenceNumber": null,
        "serverEncrypted": false
      },
      "snapshot": null
    }
  ],
  "next_marker": "2!76!MDAwMDEzIWZpbGVfMTAxNy50eHQhMDAwMDI4ITk5OTktMTItMzFUMjM6NTk6NTkuOTk5OTk5OVoh"
}
```
Pro: the most completed and concise return result. friendly to JMESPath query.
Con: it's a breaking change.

#### Solution 2:
Keep the list structure, attach the next marker to each blob info.
```JSON
[
    {
      "content": null,
      "metadata": null,
      "name": "file_1016.txt",
      "properties": {
        "appendBlobCommittedBlockCount": null,
        "blobType": "BlockBlob",
        "contentLength": 264,
        "contentRange": null,
        "contentSettings": {
          "cacheControl": null,
          "contentDisposition": null,
          "contentEncoding": null,
          "contentLanguage": null,
          "contentMd5": "lMf6lT+DBK3I6k9YdydOgg==",
          "contentType": "application/octet-stream"
        },
        "copy": {
          "completionTime": null,
          "id": null,
          "progress": null,
          "source": null,
          "status": null,
          "statusDescription": null
        },
        "etag": "0x8D4B758188BCA6C",
        "lastModified": "2017-06-19T21:13:56+00:00",
        "lease": {
          "duration": null,
          "state": "available",
          "status": "unlocked"
        },
        "pageBlobSequenceNumber": null,
        "serverEncrypted": false
      },
      "snapshot": null,
      "next_marker": "2!76!MDAwMDEzIWZpbGVfMTAxNy50eHQhMDAwMDI4ITk5OTktMTItMzFUMjM6NTk6NTkuOTk5OTk5OVoh"
    }
]
```
Pro: not a breaking change. keep the existing structure.
Con: redundant data in output. making query next marker a bit awkward in JMESPath.

#### Solution 3:
Remove the `--result-nums` and `--marker` parameter from CLI command. Since the `ListGenerator` is already doing server side paging, we can leave it to user for the client side paging.

issue_title: Replace istr()
issue_body:
Deriving from `str` is too expensive.
We need fast and simple way to figure out is key already titled (canonized) or not.

The alternative could be `multidict.canonize(s: str) -> str` function.
It converts parameter to `s.title()`, intern it by `sys.intern(...)` call and store the result into global dictionary.
Thus we have a very fast check for  canonized strings.

The drawback or proposal is the canonized strings are never deleted (I think it's fine for `aiohttp.hdrs` usage). All `canonize()` calls should be done on module import stage, that's why I propose a new function  instead of adding new tricks to `istr`.

`istr` will be supported for years anyway but `canonize` is the only way to keep multidicts fast.
Right now `istr` calls is 10-25% slower on `MultiDict` -- its not acceptable (but it's still much faster on `CIMultiDict` lookups than plain `str`).

issue_title: Complexity costs
issue_body: The nbgrader system is complex enough that when something goes wrong, the average instructor won't have any idea how to recover without writing lots of custom python code. issue_title: Inconsistent Serialization of the Session Identifier
Integration with RELATE
issue_body:
I have written some online widgetry to support my classes. That system is called "RELATE", you can take a look here if interested:

https://github.com/inducer/relate

The basic "unit of teaching" there is called a "flow", and it presents a sequence of pages to the learner. What's on a page is pretty flexible--there are lots of options (multiple choice, python code, pdf submission, ...). The Python code submission experience could be better though, since every time a learner clicks "submit", a Docker container needs to be spun up, which leads to a 1-3 second delay, which is just enough to disrupt the nice "immediacy" that Python usually provides.

It would be really slick (at least IMO) if a Jupyter notebook could be offered up as one of the page types, along with some server-side logistics to spin up the containers running the kernels.

To my mind, Relate and Jupyter complement each other very nicely in a teaching setting. Relate's "run python" capability is basic, but it has a good story for authoring, data retention, access control to course content, and exam proctoring. Jupyter hits the opposite spot: It provides a great coding experience, but the classroom integration features are pretty basic.

So a first, basic question from my side would be: How friendly is the Jupyter codebase towards being embedded on the server and client sides (Relate is Django on the server side, but that can proxy for any wsgi app. Websockets I don't know so much about, but I assume that's likely solvable, too.) A simple client-side integration story would be to just let Jupyter manage a whole page (or an iframe) and hook into its "save" functionality. For what it's worth, the use case I have in mind is ~200-300 students in a classroom, all hacking away in their own notebooks. As in, I'd like the fan-out to individual little Docker containers on a zoo of backends to happen at the web worker level, but the scale is such that it'd likely be tricky to maintain a worker process per logged-in user.

I'd love to hear your thoughts on the feasibility of this. Also, if you're interested in devoting some resources to this, I'd love to chat--but that's not primarily what I'm asking about.

issue_title: Custom Sonnet module fails to merit device placement
issue_body:
Recently I try to write some customized python modules based on Sonnet for processing EEG data, but due to the magnitude of EEG data, I want to utilize multiple GPUs for training, then problem emerges that I cannot figure it out.

According to my understanding, the paradigm for synchronous multi-GPU training requires the trainable variables resides on CPU, while operations are performed on GPU, and tensors are transported in between as required.

The problem is in my view such an arrangement is difficult for the Sonnet module. For example, considering the small module as follows:

import tensorflow as tf
import sonnet as snt

class RCNNOutput(snt.AbstractModule):
    def __init__(self, out_size, device_name, name = "rcnn_output"):
        super(RCNNOutput, self).__init__(name = name)
        with self._enter_variable_scope():
            with tf.device(device_name):
                bf = snt.BatchFlatten(name = "bf")
                fc0 = snt.Linear(output_size = 256, name = "fc0")
                fc1 = snt.Linear(output_size = out_size, name = "fc1")
                self._seq = snt.Sequential([bf, fc0, tf.nn.relu, fc1], name = "seq")

    def _build(self, inputs):
        return self._seq(inputs)

def test():
    import numpy as np

    rcnn_output = RCNNOutput(4, '/cpu:0')

    with tf.device('/cpu:0'):
        t = tf.constant(np.ones([8, 32, 32, 3]), np.float32)

    with tf.device('/gpu:0'):
        outputs = rcnn_output(t)

    writer = tf.summary.FileWriter("rcnn_output_output", tf.get_default_graph())

    config = tf.ConfigProto(log_device_placement = True)

    with tf.Session(config = config) as sess:
        sess.run([tf.global_variables_initializer(), tf.local_variables_initializer()])

        v = sess.run(outputs)
        print(v)

    writer.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test()


whatever I tried, I cannot get the weights put on CPU, it always put on GPU, even I explicitly give the placement directive:

2017-08-31 21:51:11.250360: I tensorflow/core/common_runtime/simple_placer.cc:847] rcnn_output/fc1/b: (VariableV2)/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/gpu:0

handler.document.toprettyxml()
```

issue_body:
I have a geojson that I would like to map, namely [this one](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/codeforamerica/click_that_hood/master/public/data/manhattan.geojson). When I plot it using

issue_title: Python vs curl response times
issue_body:
I am seeing very slow response times on API requests to gateway.watsonplatform.net via Python.

As an example:

- A [Get information about a Classifier](https://www.ibm.com/watson/developercloud/natural-language-classifier/api/v1/curl.html?curl#get-information-about-a-classifier) request to Watson NLC with Python takes **75-150 seconds** to respond.

- The same request with Curl responds instantaneously.

- The problem is evident when calling other services; e.g., Watson NLU.

- The problem is evident only when calling the endpoint _https://gateway.watsonplatform.net/natural-language-classifier/api_ .   The problem is _not_ evident when calling the API Explorer _https://watson-api-explorer.mybluemix.net/apis/natural-language-classifier-v1_ .

- This is a recent development, first noticed about one week ago.

Please see the attached scripts for examples.

The only combination of factors that reproducibly returns a slow response is a Python call to the default endpoint gateway.watsonplatform.net .

Are there any known issues, or recent changes to the environment that might cause the slow response?

#### python sdk version

issue_title: Hex mistyped in Windows 'nt' implementation of miniterm
issue_body:
At line 126 in miniterm.py the hex value 0x0e should actually read 0xe0

https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/b178d12fece4d87e40d2894f542063bfe5ead7bd/serial/tools/miniterm.py#L126

The current value doesn't filter character sequences as it should (like arrow up, arrow down, page up, page down, home, end which are prefixed with a byte with integer value 224 , following https://www.computerhope.com/ansisys.htm). 

This means nav keys are sent through directly making junk characters on the terminal.

issue_title: Z3 very slow on simple floating-point VC
issue_body:
We are trying to use Z3 to prove critical code involving floating-point arithmetic, and we've hit a few cases where Z3 is too slow to be usable, even on small VCs with "simple" (if this can be defined!) properties, like monotonicity of addition here. I'm attaching a manually crafted VC reproducing the problem, which a very recent snapshot of Z3 (Nov 21 2016) takes 8 minutes on a modern machine (Core i7 with 2,8 GHz and 16 GB RAM) to prove 'unsat'. We would very much appreciate any recommendation on how to use Z3 to prove such properties (use specific switches? add specific assertions?), and any explanation that helps us decide when a property will not be provable by Z3 in a reasonable time due to the algorithms used.

To understand the VC below:
We have that x1 and x2 are in the range -2,000,000 .. 2,000,000
and that y is between -1 and 1 (all in floats)
and that x1 is less than or equal to x2
and we would like to prove that (x1 + y) is less than or equal to (x2 + y)

The calls to function 'finite' are probably useless, but were added in the hope of helping Z3.

CVC4 proves this property immediately (with a different encoding as CVC4 does not support natively floating-point numbers).

(set-logic QF_FPBV)
(set-info :source |SPARK inspired floating point problems|)
(set-info :smt-lib-version 2.5)
(set-info :category crafted)
(set-info :status unsat)

(define-const low_bound  Float32 ((_ to_fp 8 24) RNE (- 2000000.0)))
(define-const high_bound Float32 ((_ to_fp 8 24) RNE 2000000.0))

(define-const minus_one  Float32 ((_ to_fp 8 24) RNE (- 1.0)))
(define-const one        Float32 ((_ to_fp 8 24) RNE 1.0))

(define-fun finite ((x Float32)) Bool
  (or (fp.isZero x) (fp.isNormal x) (fp.isSubnormal x)))

;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;
;; Parameters
;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;

(declare-const x1 Float32)
(assert (fp.geq x1 low_bound))
(assert (fp.leq x1 high_bound))
(assert (finite x1))

(declare-const x2 Float32)
(assert (fp.geq x2 low_bound))
(assert (fp.leq x2 high_bound))
(assert (finite x2))

(declare-const y Float32)
(assert (fp.geq y minus_one))
(assert (fp.leq y one))
(assert (finite y))

(assert (fp.leq x1 x2))

;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;
;; Implementation
;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;

(define-const new_x1 Float32 (fp.add RNE x1 y))
(define-const new_x2 Float32 (fp.add RNE x2 y))

(assert (finite new_x1))
(assert (finite new_x2))

(assert (not (fp.leq new_x1 new_x2)))
(check-sat)
(exit)

issue_title: z3Py generates wrong counterexample (BitVectors)
issue_body:
```
from z3 import *

def min(x, y):
  if simplify(ULT(x, y)):
    return x
  else:
    return y

def max(x, y): 
  if simplify(ULT(x, y)):
    return y
  else:
    return x

def div(n, k): 
  return simplify(UDiv(n, k)) 


def divide(n, k): 
  l = BitVec('l', 32) 
  m_ = BitVec('m_', 64) 
  one = BitVecVal(1, 64) 
  one32 = BitVecVal(1, 32) 
  zero = BitVecVal(0, 32) 
  q = BitVec('q', 32) 
  t = BitVec('t', 64) 
  sh1 = BitVec('sh1', 32) 
  sh2 = BitVec('sh2', 32) 
  s = Solver() 
  s.add(And(ULE(k, (1 << l)), UGT(k, (1 << (l - 1)))))
  if s.check() == sat:
    m = s.model()
    l = m.evaluate(l)
  m_ =  ((one << 32) * ((one << ZeroExt(32, l)) - ZeroExt(32, k)) / ZeroExt(32, k)) + 1 
  sh1 = min(l, one32)
  sh2 = max(l - one32, zero)
  t = LShR((ZeroExt(32, n) * m_), 32) 
  n64 = ZeroExt(32, n)
  sh164 = ZeroExt(32, sh1)
  sh264 = ZeroExt(32, sh2)
  q = Extract(31, 0, LShR((t + (LShR((n64 - t), sh164))), sh264))
  return simplify(q)

n = BitVec('n', 32) 
k = BitVec('k', 32)

prove(Or(k == 0, div(n, k) == divide(n, k)))

print()

# prove that counterexample is wrong
n_ = BitVecVal(2147483647, 32)
k_ = BitVecVal(536870912, 32)
prove(div(n_,k_) == divide(n_,k_))
```

On my PC it generates the counterexample `n = 2147483647` and `k = 536870912` which when I check by applying `div(n, k)` and `divide(n, k)` ― both functions give the same result. 


I was trying to prove that the optimization described in figure 4.1 of [Division by Invariant Integers using Multiplication](https://gmplib.org/~tege/divcnst-pldi94.pdf) is correct.

issue_title: Z3 4.7.1 segfault at theory_bv.cpp:1394 during Augur contracts analysis
issue_body:
Oyente, Ethereum smart contracts analysis tool, which runs Z3 solver under the hood fails with a Z3 segfault when Z3 solver is upgraded to 4.7.1 and Oyente tool is analysing Augur smart contracts.

The segfault does not happen on Z3 4.5.0 and 4.6.0.

Steps to reproduce:

Those commands run Oyente with 4.5.0 solver:

```
 $  git clone https://github.com/cryptomental/augur-core.git
 $  cd augur-core/
 $  git co feature/ignore-selected-oyente-issues
 $  npm run docker:run:test:security:oyente
```

Switching to 4.7.1 can be done with the following patch in source/support/test/oyente/Dockerfile :

```
diff --git a/source/support/test/oyente/Dockerfile b/source/support/test/oyente/Dockerfile
index e12061d5..43acfb23 100644
--- a/source/support/test/oyente/Dockerfile
+++ b/source/support/test/oyente/Dockerfile
@@ -11,9 +11,9 @@ RUN apt-get update \
        && apt-get install -y software-properties-common \
        && apt-get install -y unzip
 
-RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/archive/z3-4.5.0.zip \
-       && unzip z3-4.5.0.zip -d /tmp \
-       && cd /tmp/z3-z3-4.5.0 \
+RUN wget --quiet https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/archive/z3-4.7.1.zip \
+       && unzip z3-4.7.1.zip -d /tmp \
+       && cd /tmp/z3-z3-4.7.1 \
        && python scripts/mk_make.py --python \
        && cd build \
        && make -j8 \
```

https://gist.github.com/cryptomental/c5ff857becfeda48c8c0fbb432eeba90

Verbose Oyente log including ASM dump:

https://gist.github.com/cryptomental/04e70f3b849573980814a29aa45134ac

issue_body:
I am using Daniel's facial key-point recognition tutorial as a template for a personal project, and I'm just at the point of training my model but am running into a puzzling error. As in the [tutorial](https://github.com/dnouri/kfkd-tutorial/blob/master/kfkd.py#L407), I am training multiple specialist models using a for loop:

``` python
def fit_specialists(fname_pretrain = None):
    # Fit specialist models running on the GPU
    a = 1
    for setting in SPECIALIST_SETTINGS:
        # Load the appropriate specialist data
        cols = setting['columns']
        flip_indices = setting['flip_indices']
        X,y = load2d(cols=cols,flip_inds=flip_indices)

        # Create the model
        model = clone(net)
        model.output_num_units = y.shape[1]
        model.max_epochs = 1 # !!! int(4e6 / y.shape[0])
        if 'kwargs' in setting:
            # an option 'kwargs' in the settings list may be used to
            # set any other parameter of the net:
            vars(model).update(setting['kwargs'])

        if 'fname_pretrain' in setting:
            fname_pretrain = setting['fname_pretrain']

        if fname_pretrain is not None:
            model.load_params_from(fname_pretrain)

        print("Training model for columns {} for {} epochs".format(
            cols, model.max_epochs))
        print "model.fit about to start"
        model.fit(X, y)
        print "model.fit stopped"
        train_loss = np.array([i["train_loss"] for i in simple_net.train_history_])
        valid_loss = np.array([i["valid_loss"] for i in simple_net.train_history_])
        plt.plot(train_loss, linewidth=3, label="train")
        plt.plot(valid_loss, linewidth=3, label="valid")
        plt.grid()
        plt.legend()
        tit = "Model " + num2str(a) + " Loss"

        plt.xlabel("epoch")
        plt.ylabel("loss")
        plt.yscale("log")
        chart_name = "Model" + num2str(a) + "Loss.png"
        plt.savefig(chart_name)
        name = str(a)+'_params.pkl'

    hidden4_num_units=1000,

    hidden5_num_units=1000,
    output_num_units=30, output_nonlinearity=None,

    update_learning_rate=theano.shared(float32(0.03)),

issue_title: Recommended practice for adding reserved characters?
issue_body:
Per @markrwilliams comment [here](https://github.com/python-hyper/hyperlink/issues/6#issuecomment-338984058) and a few others dotted around, we're facing a design gap in hyperlink's APIs. 

To paraphrase:
```
url = URL()
url.add(u'param', u'#value').to_text()
```
Yields:
```
ValueError: one or more reserved delimiters &# present in query parameter value: u'#value'
```

This is due to a subtle shift in hyperlink's design compared to `twisted.python.url`. `t.p.url` would allow any string value in, whereas hyperlink prefers to store the "minimally-encoded" version. This is why a `ValueError` is raised from the code above.

Technically, this can be solved by making the code `url.add(u'param', _encode_query_part(u'#value'))`. But hyperlink's primary goal is to handle encoding/decoding, does it really make sense to push that back on the user?

One solution Mark and I discussed would be to switch to decoding every value passed in. But what if someone were to pass in `u'%23%'` and actually intend for that to be their raw value. Autodecoding might have better usability most of the time, but much like relying on Python 2's implicit encoding/decoding, the safety of the explicit `_encode_*_part()` is probably preferable.

It might occur to one that this entire problem bears some resemblance to the `bytes`/`unicode` split, as `URL` has `URL.to_uri()` and `URL.to_iri()`. There is some truth to this, but both IRIs and URIs are both URLs. Having two types imposes a sort of artificial split I'd like to avoid if possible, but we also don't have a good way to represent an already decoded IRI. This was causing an issue with double decoding on multiple `.to_iri()` calls (see #16). 

Right now my best idea is to enable that technical solution above by exposing the various encoding and decoding functions as public APIs, since those may prove useful utilities for other contexts anyways. I'm sure there are better ideas, too, so I'm going to leave this issue open as a place for discussion on handling this quandary.

issue_title: IPython on AFS
issue_body:
Using Ganga with IPython pointing to the home directory for the history causes the following error when you have multiple sessions:

```The history saving thread hit an unexpected error (DatabaseError('database disk image is malformed',)).History will not be written to the database.```

This isn't the end of the world but I imagine some users could become irate over this. Maybe we could consider putting the live-history in a temp space and copying it over as part of the Ganga shutdown process (potentially allowing us to merge histories if sessions have overlapped in a nice way. i.e. 1 session writes to the history completely after another).

issue_title: context_value passed to schema.execute should not be dictionary
issue_body:
It seems that the all the documentation that uses `context_value` is supplying a dictionary for the value.  For example, from docs/testing/index.rst , the line

        executed = client.execute('''{ hey }''', context_value={'user': 'Peter'})

I believe that this incorrect -- the value `context_value` should actually be an object with an attribute user.  For example, this is working for me instead:

    from django.test import RequestFactory
    request_factory = RequestFactory()
    my_request = request_factory.get('/api/')
    my_request.user = User.objects.first() # randomly picking a real user
    executed = client.execute('''{ hey }''', context_value=my_request)

I am using RequestFactory to create a WSGIRequest object, which I believe is what the `execute` method needs instead of a dictionary for the `context_value` parameter, at least when using Django.

If someone can confirm that I am correct, I'll create the pull request to fix the documentation.

Add `sorl_thumbnail_tags` templatetag library that mirrors traditional `thumbnail`.

This allows usage of sorl-thumbnail in templates by {% load sorl_thumbnail_tags %} instead of traditional {% load thumbnail %}. It's specifically useful in projects that do make use of multiple thumbnailer libraries (for instance `easy-thumbnails` alongside `sorl-thumbnail`).

Works fine in my [fork](https://github.com/barseghyanartur/sorl-thumbnail/blob/master/sorl/thumbnail/templatetags/sorl_thumbnail_tags.py). If you, in principle, have nothing against addition of `sorl_thumbnail_tags` mirror, I'll add tests and update the documentation prior making a pull request.

        <script charset="utf-8" type="text/javascript" src="{% static 'bootstrap/dist/js/bootstrap.js' %}"></script>
    {% endcompress %}
``` 

And the versions are as follows (extracted from `bower.json`):

  "dependencies": {

    "jquery-ui": "^1.11.4",
    "lodash": "^4.13.1",
    "minilog": "^3.0.0"

```

The rJSMin compressor crashed the template load whenever the `{% compress js %}` tag was present, but SlimIt compressor did not have any problems whatsoever with any of the templates.

The `minilog.js` file seems to be already minified when fed to the compressor, though it does not have the `.min.js` suffix.

issue_title: It should be possible to follow_link by text as part of the common api
issue_body:
PyQuery supports the deprecated CSS3/JQuery extensions :contains(), and FuncSeleniumTest.click() can take an argument (text=...), so it should be possible to follow_link by the link's text, which in the absence of an id or class on the element itself is often the most stable way to select and element (rather than depending on the tree structure using x > y and :nth-child).

In general, it should be possible to support xpath too also since PyQuery is implemented by compiling CSS selectors to xpath.

What do you think the api should look like? Perhaps we could just extend the signature of follow_link(...) to be like click(...)?

issue_title: Support SparkPost EU
issue_body:
SparkPost has [announced SparkPost EU](https://www.sparkpost.com/blog/announcing-sparkpost-eu/), a version of their service hosted entirely in the European Union. Anymail should add a setting to allow using the EU service as an alternative to the US-based default.

It looks like this just involves pointing the SparkPost API at the EU [API endpoint](https://developers.sparkpost.com/api/index.html#header-api-endpoints). The `sparkpost.SparkPost` constructor has an undocumented [`base_uri` parameter](https://github.com/SparkPost/python-sparkpost/blob/v1.3.6/sparkpost/__init__.py#L18) that seems to allow this. 

The new Anymail setting should probably be SPARKPOST_API_URL (rather than _URI), paralleling similar settings in other backends.

This problem will go away (for now) by solving #2208, but the way we've currently been handling conditional dependencies is by dynamically setting `install_requires` in `setup.py`. This causes our wheels to be built incorrectly, however, which while it doesn't negatively affect OS or `certbot-auto` packaging, it might be nice to get this right.

If we do this we should get a new enough version of `setuptools` available in `pipstrap` and verify with our Debian, EPEL, and Ubuntu maintainers that this won't cause issues for them.

issue_title: Create leaf.pem instead of cert.pem for new lineages
issue_body:
We see a fairly large number of sites misconfigured to serve only the end-entity (leaf) certificate, not the entire chain. I believe part of this is that we create a file `cert.pem` in addition to `fullchain.pem`. For someone looking to manually install a certificate, `cert.pem` looks like the natural choice. We can help people configure their sites correctly by renaming `cert.pem` to something else, like leaf.pem. This should be accompanied by a README in the certificates directory explaining what each file is.

We don't want to disrupt existing lineages where people are depending on certain naming conventions, but can can have Certbot take this new behavior for new lineages.

I realize this is a fairly disruptive change, but since part of our goal is to remove the human error and pain points in deploying certificates, it seems worthwhile.

issue_title: restful() should handle non-200 success codes
issue_body: There are endpoints in the Salesforce API that result in non `200` response codes (i.e. 201 from [upserts](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/dome_upsert.htm)). Currently, `restful()` will result in an exception being raised if the response code is [not strictly `200`](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce/blob/ef0a9487a6cd4854466d00969c32da000d34392a/simple_salesforce/api.py#L271). This should be updated to accept anything outside of obvious error code ranges (`4xx` and `5xx`).

issue_title: Include timestamp in internal events
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Right now, the internal raiden events only includes an increasing ID, which is enough for ordering, but not very useful for clients, specially the webUI events views. The ID is also enough for clients which keeps polling for the data, but doesn't help clients processing historical events (for any reason). Blockchain events have the block numbers, which have an associated timestamp, but internal events don't.
We should also include the `timestamp` on each event, set on the moment it was processed by the WAL, and expose it in all events APIs. The UI can then use it to show a human-readable time.

PS: this could be done in a non-breaking way, by letting the current data as null, or doing a db upgrade function to interleave the block events, but we can avoid doing that by queuing it for merge on the next breaking change release.

issue_title: .NET Support
issue_body:
In March I [prototyped](https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/tree/ellismg/add-dotnet) a small language provider built for .NET, using CSharp Interactive. I got far enough to be able to create resources from C# by invoking the resource providers "directly".  Very raw, but enough to show promise.

Since launch, it's become clear that folks would love to see more first class .NET support.  So let's dust this off and have a go at it.

There's a lot to do:

[ ] Make sure it builds and you can actually run it.  Pulumi has changed a lot of in the past three months, so there may be some dusting off.
[ ] Move away from the CSI based implementation.  While using CSI was a great way to demo, I don't think its tenable to build on top of that long term.  Instead, we'll want to do something like what we did in golang (a top level `Pulumi.Run()` method that you pass a function to and from there you can do Pulumi things?  Or maybe we have some convention where you define a static method like Deploy() which returns a Task, and Pulumi uses reflection to call that.  Doing it this way also means that any .NET language can play along.
[ ] tfgen support.  When we do this, we'll have to figure out how we want to represent the fundamental async nature of resource creation.  I think the Input/Output model we have in the javascript language host is the right abstraction.  Outputs are basically wrappers over Tasks.
[ ] Closure serialization.  The compiler actually does a lot of work for us here and I think we can leverage reflection to understand the values of closed over things and then generate the glue code to re-hydrate the proxies with their correct values at runtime.

I'll be working on this over the next few days.  If folks want to pitch in, please join the #contribute channel on https://slack.pulumi.com/ and ping `@matt`.

issue_title: [AWS Autoscaler] create_node hangs forever when insufficient AWS Capacity
issue_body:
In aws/node_provider.py:create_node, the call to ```self.ec2.create_instances``` can block for over half an hour, stopping all other Monitor activity. This has happened to me for spot requests when AWS has no capacity, but it could plausibly happen in other situations too.

A quick fix might be to reduce the number of retries that boto makes. They support a [global parameter](http://botocore.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/config.html), but it doesn't seem like it can be set per request.

A more robust approach would be to use request_spot_instances, reserving create_instance only for on-demand instances; however, this would complicate the code significantly, as I think we'd need to poll for when the instances are created.

Alternately, we could switch to using {request,modify}_spot_fleet, which supports both on-demand and spot types. I think this is closest to how Amazon intends the API to be used, but it is a bit of a mismatch with the Ray autoscaling API. The main problem is in scaling down: I think we'd need to follow [this procedure](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45870613/terminate-specific-instance-in-aws-spot-fleet) to terminate a specific node. We'd also still need to poll to discover new nodes.

### Description
I personally use streamlink for all my stream needs (mostly twitch), so I type a lot of streamlink commands into the terminal so any less typing would be appreciated.

`streamlink twitch.tv/pgl_dota`

defaults to

instead of just printing the stream qualities

issue_title: [V3 Bank] bank.withdraw/deposit_credits breaks mongodb with floats
issue_body:
# Other bugs

If you give bank.deposit_credits or bank.withdraw_credit a float instead of an int while using MongoDB, the users balance is stuck as a double. Because of this many cogs break because they expect an int, but get a float.

#### What were you trying to do?

I was trying to withdraw credits from a users account with

`await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)`

although due to a bug I accidentaly ran

```
await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
await bank.deposit_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
````

This is most likely unrelated to the bug, but I thought I should mention it anyway.

#### What were you expecting to happen?

I expected red to either throw an error or convert the float to an int 

#### What actually happened?

Red carried on, as if the value was an int. It changed the value type of the balance in mongodb from an int32 to a double. This breaks many other cogs because they expect an int but get a double.

#### How can we reproduce this issue?

1. run the following command

```
await bank.withdraw_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
await bank.deposit_credits(ctx.author, X * 0.30)
```

2. done

I'm more than happy to write docstrings for the Selection widgets if we think it's useful.

issue_title: Span References
issue_body:
<!--
Welcome to the Jaeger project! 👋🎉

- Please search for existing issues to avoid creating duplicate bugs/feature requests.
- Please be respectful and considerate of others when commenting on issues.
- Please provide as much information as possible so we all understand the issue.
- If you only have a question, you may get a faster response by asking in
    - our chat room https://gitter.im/jaegertracing/Lobby, or
    - the forum https://groups.google.com/d/forum/jaeger-tracing
    (but please don't double post)
-->

## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

We are currently working on tracing a distributed system that uses message queues between services to process events. The goal is to trace individual events through the system, showing when they were put into the queue, when they were dequeued, what occurred during their processing, and so on.

Some steps in the system involve batch processing. e.g. - 1,000 events will be taken out of the queue, all processed together, and inserted into downstream queues.

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?

The problem we're running into is how to model these batch operations. Each event has a trace associated with it, and ideally these batch operations would show up in traces for all events that were in the batch. Conceptually, what I'd like to do is create one span for the batch processing (and perhaps some child spans within it) - and associate it as a child of all the traces of events in the batch.

It seems like the opentracing API might support this type of use case via references. However the Python library doesn't currently support them - instead it just picks the first reference and uses it as the parent.

I am curious if jaeger backend supports using references in this way. If I were to create a span with multiple references, would it show up in all of those traces in the UI? Further - is there any practical difference between using `CHILD_OF` and `FOLLOWS_FROM`?

I understand that the span would need to pick one of them to use for its trace id, but I am not too concerned about that for now.

## Proposal - what do you suggest to solve the problem or improve the existing situation?

If this is already supported in the backend - maybe as simple as including the reference information in the thrift messages?

## Any open questions to address

<!-- Questions that should be answered before proceeding with implementation. -->

issue_title: Using a "bot" to copy a merge request to several branches with python gitlab - author of merge request
issue_body:
Hi, 

in our projects branching model and worfklow we often require developer to submit a merge request of his dev-branch to several branches upstream, say master, and release-branch. 

In order for developer himself not to have to make several merge requests, I am developing a system where developer instead labels his merge request with the other branches he wants this dev-branch to be merged into. 

Once a merge request is submitted in the gitlab upstream repo, a webhook is sent, and a script is called duplicating this original merge request and creating one or several new ones depending on the situation. 

The problem is that the merge request author of the new copied merge requests is automatically set to be the user submitting the request through the api, which for us is called  nordugrid-arc-bot. I would like the author to be the same as the original author, or at least include this person as a participant in order for this person to be linked with the merge request, and handle merge conflicts and so on. 

I have tried to set the author to be the original merge requests author, but it seems to be overwritten:

In my python-script that is called by exec of the webhook application:
```
#extracting original author
author = this_mr.author

#creating new mr_dictionary
mr_dict =  {'author':author,
                'source_branch': source_branch,
                'source_project_id': project_id,
                'target_branch': target,
                'target_project_id': project_id,
                'title': title,
                'description':descr + 'author.id: ' + str(author.id) + 'author.name: ' + str(author.name),
                'milestone':this_mr.milestone,
                'labels': ['arcbot','copied:'+target]}
```

After the mr is submitted, I see 

```
mr id:  163 	mr_iid:  46 	author:  <class 'gitlab.v3.objects.User'> => {'_module': <module 'gitlab.v3.objects' from '/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/python_gitlab-1.2.0-py2.7.egg/gitlab/v3/objects.pyc'>, u'username': u'arcbot', u'name': u'nordugrid-arc-bot', u'avatar_url': u'https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/37272d389e04cb26a342dda1b7156ab5?s=80&d=identicon', 'gitlab': <gitlab.Gitlab object at 0x10924c950>, '_from_api': True, u'state': u'active', u'web_url': u'https://source.coderefinery.org/arcbot', 'project_id': 205, u'id': 112}
```

See the duplicate mr here: 
https://source.coderefinery.org/maikenp/arc-test-gitlab-ci/merge_requests/46

(sorry, deleted the example, but you can see similar example here: https://source.coderefinery.org/maikenp/arc-test-gitlab-ci/merge_requests/2)

So it seems the author is overwritten by the submitter of the merge request, which in this case is nordugrid-arc-bot

Am I supposed to be able to set the author of a merge request by hand? 

If not, is there some other workaround that could work for me? 

Thanks!
Maiken

issue_title: Improvement TensorDB : remove hard dependency on mongoDB  and make it loose.
issue_body:
Given the general philosophy of TensorDB to connect DB based on query,
to remove strong dependency on DB framework.
TensorDB : Astract layer which encapsulates DB connection :  (Stream data, large Blob data, ...).

Having dependency on MongoDB is a pain, 
because we may use other DB framework: Big Query, Postgres, MySQL, Aurora, ....

It would be better to have general abstract layer and add specification for the DB platform
through engine definition (like in SQL Alchemy):
http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/core/engines.html

engine= create_engine(type="MongoDB", connection_string="   ")
engine= create_engine(type="filesystem", connection_string="   ")
engine= create_engine(type="AWSAurora", connection_string="   ")

db= TensorDB(engine=engine, .... )
...

It allows to add new connector without the need of changing the abstraction layer,
like in SQL Alchemy.

So, you can mix NO SQL DB with SQL DB as well as filesystem

issue_title: Tag annotations ignored when multiple are present
issue_body:
The following line from the Ensembl Homo_sapiens.GRCh37.75.gtf  is parsed incorrectly:

```
1   protein_coding  exon    860260  860328  .   +   .   gene_id "ENSG00000187634"; transcript_id "ENST00000420190"; exon_number "1"; gene_name "SAMD11"; gene_source "ensembl_havana"; gene_biotype "protein_coding"; transcript_name "SAMD11-011"; transcript_source "havana"; exon_id "ENSE00001637883"; tag "cds_end_NF"; tag "mRNA_end_NF";
```

As you can see the line contains two 'tag' attributes. Only the second one is present in the DataFrame returned by `read_gtf_as_dataframe`:

```
seqname          source feature   start     end  score strand frame  \
0       1  protein_coding    exon  860260  860328    NaN      +     .

           gene_id    transcript_id exon_number gene_name     gene_source  \
0  ENSG00000187634  ENST00000420190           1    SAMD11  ensembl_havana

     gene_biotype transcript_name transcript_source          exon_id  \
0  protein_coding      SAMD11-011            havana  ENSE00001637883

           tag
0  mRNA_end_NF
```

The `cds_end_NF` tag is lost. Ideally both tags should be presented in a list, but I'm not sure if that's possible with pandas.

issue_title: clients able to manage broker side resources...
issue_body:
query exchanges available, queue status, bindings.

would like to add some meta queries so that a user can ask a broker which exchanges can it write to, and which exchanges can it read from.

the mechanism to implement, is that a post is done to the user's xs_<user> exchange.
some daemon picks up the message, and generates a response, and puts it in xl_<user>.

likely results in a new message format v02.control ?
...
maybe just a single query that, for any given user, returns:

-- exchanges to which a user can post.
-- queues to which a user can connect/subscribe.
-- the number of messages in each of those queues.
...

 added rabbitmq_user_access routine to sr_rabbit, which returns the requisite list.

I think a plugin could be written to handle requests, call this routine, and write a response posting to queue given in the request.


 sr_ctl
declare exchange hoho
new_passwd lala
list (all exchanges, queues, and bindings)
declare queue
bind queue ?
delete binding.
delete queue

This is an import of https://sourceforge.net/p/metpx/feature-requests/60/

issue_title: lark does not tokenize properly
issue_body:
I have the following grammar:

```python
parser = Lark(r'''
input                : word+
word                 : unquoted | lquoted | squoted | dquoted

lquoted               : /\$'[^']*'/
squoted               : /'[^']*'/
dquoted               : /"(?:[^"]+|\\.)*"/
unquoted              : /(?:[^\s()&;|<>]+|\\.)+/

%import common.WS
%ignore WS
''', start='input', parser='lalr')
```

When I try to parse a string like `$'abcd'`, this is what I get:

```
> $'abcd'
input
  word
    unquoted	$'abcd'
```

However, on the basis of the rules, shouldn't the result yield a `lquoted` instead of a `unquoted`?
